title: Sandwich Idada
description: An iconic snack from Surat is a perfect choice for your Holi celebrations. This soft, spongy and subtly flavoured dhokla which will bring a smile with every bite and will become a favourite with whoever eats it.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
main_ingredients: Split skinless black gram (dhuli urad dal)

ingredients.raw: 1½ cups short grain rice, soaked for 1 hour and drained
ingredients.name: short grain rice, soaked for 1 hour and drained

ingredients.raw: ½ cup split skinless black gram (dhuli urad dal), soaked for 1 hour and drained
ingredients.name: ½ cup split skinless black gram , soaked for 1 hour and drained

ingredients.raw: ½ cup thick pressed rice (jada poha), soaked for 10 minutes and drained
ingredients.name: ½ cup thick pressed rice , soaked for 10 minutes and drained

ingredients.raw: 1 teaspoon fenugreek seeds (methi dana), soaked for 1 hour and drained
ingredients.name: fenugreek seeds , soaked for 1 hour and drained

ingredients.raw: Tamatar moongfalli ki chutney as required
ingredients.name: Tamatar moongfalli ki chutney as required

ingredients.raw: Chopped fresh coriander leaves for garnish
ingredients.name: Chopped fresh coriander leaves for garnish

steps: Put rice in a grinder jar, add split black gram, pressed rice, fenugreek seeds, yogurt and asafoetida and grind coarsely.
Add ¾ cup water and continue to grind to a fine batter. Transfer into a bowl, cover and set aside to ferment for 8-10 hours.
Add ginger-green chilli paste, salt and sugar and mix well. Add fruit salt and mix well.
Heat sufficient water in a steamer. Grease dhokla plates with oil and pour a portion of batter in each plate.
Arrange the plates in the steamer, sprinkle crushed black peppercorns and red chilli powder on top and steam for 6-8 minutes.
Demould, apply some oil on top and allow to cool.
Place the plain side of 1 disc facing upwards and spread tamatar moongfalli ki chutney evenly. Place another disc on top with the plain side facing down and press gently. Cut into squares.
Arrange the squares in a serving plate, garnish with coriander leaves and serve.
